title: Unit Talk V2 — Dual-Authorization Runtime Matrix
gap_closed: Gap #16 — dual authorization was convention only; now mechanically enforced at the runtime layer

dual_auth_actions.rationale: Voiding a pick is irreversible — requires two independent operator approvals.
Overriding a pick outcome affects settlement; requires dual oversight.
Mutating member records (tier, trust) requires dual authorization to prevent unilateral privilege grants.
Administrative operator actions require separation — no single operator may self-authorize.
Settlement corrections affect payouts and audit integrity; dual-auth is mandatory.
Promotion overrides bypass the automated evaluation pipeline; dual-auth prevents abuse.

lifecycle.step_1: First operator calls createPendingApproval() — produces PendingApproval record with TTL=3600s.
lifecycle.step_2: Second, independently authenticated, different operator calls completeApproval() — produces immutable ApprovalRecord.
invariants: A single approval never progresses a dual-auth action.
No operator is both first and second approver for the same action.
PendingApproval expires after TTL — no late completion allowed.
ApprovalRecord is frozen (Object.freeze) — immutable after creation.

enforcement.replay: replayApprovalChain() reconstructs the record deterministically from PendingApproval + secondApproverId + approvedAt.

adversarial_validation.scenario: Operator 'alice' creates PendingApproval; 'alice' attempts second approval — rejected with DUAL_AUTH_VIOLATION.
